architecture.summary: The system augments a subword-based language model by learning causal structures for character-level representations through interchange intervention training. A causal model treats each character as a typed variable; the framework learns interventions that map characters to robust internal representations, enabling the subword model to perform character-level reasoning (spelling, arithmetic, word games) while maintaining access to subword-level meaning and context. Character representations are learned via training interventions on the frozen subword model's activations.
trained_components: character-to-representation mappings via interchange intervention training

biblio.title: Inducing Character-level Structure in Subword-based Language Models with Type-level Interchange Intervention Training

claims.statement: Our method outperforms character-level models on more complex tasks that blend form, meaning, and context, such as spelling correction in context and word search games.
While character-level models still perform best on purely form-based tasks like string reversal, our method outperforms character-level models on more complex tasks.
Compared with standard subword-based models, our approach significantly improves robustness on unseen token sequences.
The approach leads to human-interpretable internal representations of characters.
The method treats each character as a typed variable in a causal model and learns such causal structures by adapting the interchange intervention training method of Geiger et al. (2021).
